knowledge.subject: Radiation
knowledge.description: the transfer of energy through electromagnetic waves and does not require a physical medium to travel
knowledge.source: Radiation is the transfer of energy through electromagnetic waves and does not require a physical medium to travel.

knowledge.subject: Electromagnetic waves
knowledge.description: consist of oscillating electric and magnetic fields that are perpendicular to each other and to the direction of propagation
knowledge.source: Electromagnetic waves consist of oscillating electric and magnetic fields that are perpendicular to each other and to the direction of propagation.

knowledge.subject: Radiation behavior
knowledge.description: exhibits both wave-like and particle-like behavior
knowledge.source: Radiation exhibits both wave-like and particle-like behavior, with the particle form known as photons.

knowledge.subject: Photons
knowledge.description: the particle form of radiation
knowledge.source: Radiation exhibits both wave-like and particle-like behavior, with the particle form known as photons.

knowledge.subject: Ultraviolet radiation wavelength range
knowledge.description: from 0.01 to 0.4 µm
knowledge.source: Ultraviolet radiation spans wavelengths from 0.01 to 0.4 µm.

knowledge.subject: Visible light wavelength range
knowledge.description: from 0.4 to 0.7 µm
knowledge.source: Visible light spans 0.4 to 0.7 µm.

knowledge.subject: Thermal infrared radiation wavelength range
knowledge.description: from 0.7 to 100 µm
knowledge.source: Thermal infrared radiation spans 0.7 to 100 µm.

knowledge.subject: Formula c = λf
knowledge.description: c = λf
knowledge.source: c = λf

knowledge.subject: Formula E = hf
knowledge.description: E = hf
knowledge.source: E = hf

knowledge.subject: Relationship between wavelength, frequency, and energy
knowledge.description: Shorter wavelengths correspond to higher frequencies and greater energy
knowledge.source: Shorter wavelengths correspond to higher frequencies and greater energy.

knowledge.subject: Sun's peak emission
knowledge.description: emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm
knowledge.source: The Sun emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm, while Earth emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm.

knowledge.subject: Earth's peak emission
knowledge.description: emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm
knowledge.source: The Sun emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm, while Earth emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm.

knowledge.subject: Relationship between speed of light, wavelength, and frequency
knowledge.description: c = λf
knowledge.source: The relationships between wavelength, frequency, and energy are described by: c = λf

knowledge.subject: Relationship between energy, Planck's constant, and frequency
knowledge.description: E = hf
knowledge.source: E = hf

knowledge.subject: Wavelength, frequency, and energy relationship
knowledge.description: Shorter wavelengths correspond to higher frequencies and greater energy.
knowledge.source: Shorter wavelengths correspond to higher frequencies and greater energy.

knowledge.subject: Sun's emission characteristics
knowledge.description: emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm.
knowledge.source: The Sun emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm, while Earth emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm.

knowledge.subject: Earth's emission characteristics
knowledge.description: emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm.
knowledge.source: The Sun emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm, while Earth emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm.

knowledge.subject: Blackbody
knowledge.description: an ideal object that perfectly absorbs and emits radiation based solely on its temperature.
knowledge.source: A blackbody is an ideal object that perfectly absorbs and emits radiation based solely on its temperature.

knowledge.subject: Sun's approximate temperature
knowledge.source: The Sun, with an approximate temperature of 6000 K, and Earth, with an approximate temperature of 288 K, behave similarly to blackbodies.

knowledge.subject: Earth's approximate temperature
knowledge.source: The Sun, with an approximate temperature of 6000 K, and Earth, with an approximate temperature of 288 K, behave similarly to blackbodies.

knowledge.subject: Planck's Law
knowledge.description: describes how the intensity of radiation varies with wavelength and temperature.
knowledge.source: Planck's Law describes how the intensity of radiation varies with wavelength and temperature.

knowledge.subject: Wien's Displacement Law
knowledge.description: states that the wavelength of maximum emission decreases as temperature increases.
knowledge.source: Wien's Displacement Law states that the wavelength of maximum emission decreases as temperature increases: λmax = 2897 / T

knowledge.subject: Wien's Displacement Law formula
knowledge.description: λmax = 2897 / T
knowledge.source: Wien's Displacement Law states that the wavelength of maximum emission decreases as temperature increases: λmax = 2897 / T

knowledge.subject: Stefan-Boltzmann Law
knowledge.description: states that the total energy emitted by a body is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature.
knowledge.source: Stefan-Boltzmann Law states that the total energy emitted by a body is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature: E = σT⁴

knowledge.subject: Stefan-Boltzmann Law formula
knowledge.description: E = σT⁴
knowledge.source: Stefan-Boltzmann Law states that the total energy emitted by a body is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature: E = σT⁴

knowledge.subject: Kirchhoff's Law
knowledge.description: states that a material that is an efficient absorber at a particular wavelength is also an efficient emitter at that same wavelength.
knowledge.source: Kirchhoff's Law states that a material that is an efficient absorber at a particular wavelength is also an efficient emitter at that same wavelength. Carbon dioxide is an example because it absorbs and re-emits infrared radiation.

knowledge.subject: Carbon dioxide as an example of Kirchhoff's Law
knowledge.description: absorbs and re-emits infrared radiation.
knowledge.source: Carbon dioxide is an example because it absorbs and re-emits infrared radiation.

knowledge.subject: Wavelength, frequency, and energy relationships
knowledge.description: Shorter wavelengths correspond to higher frequencies and greater energy.
knowledge.source: Shorter wavelengths correspond to higher frequencies and greater energy.

knowledge.subject: Sun's radiation emission
knowledge.description: emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm
knowledge.source: The Sun emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm, while Earth emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm.

knowledge.subject: Earth's radiation emission
knowledge.description: emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm
knowledge.source: The Sun emits primarily high-energy shortwave radiation with peak emission near 0.5 µm, while Earth emits lower-energy longwave infrared radiation with peak emission near 10 µm.

knowledge.subject: Speed of light formula
knowledge.description: c = λf
knowledge.source: c = λf

knowledge.subject: Photon energy formula
knowledge.description: E = hf
knowledge.source: E = hf

knowledge.subject: Sun's blackbody approximation
knowledge.description: The Sun, with an approximate temperature of 6000 K, ... behave similarly to blackbodies.
knowledge.source: The Sun, with an approximate temperature of 6000 K, and Earth, with an approximate temperature of 288 K, behave similarly to blackbodies.

knowledge.subject: Earth's blackbody approximation
knowledge.description: Earth, with an approximate temperature of 288 K, behave similarly to blackbodies.
knowledge.source: The Sun, with an approximate temperature of 6000 K, and Earth, with an approximate temperature of 288 K, behave similarly to blackbodies.

knowledge.subject: Carbon dioxide (Kirchhoff's Law example)
knowledge.description: absorbs and re-emits infrared radiation.
knowledge.source: Carbon dioxide is an example because it absorbs and re-emits infrared radiation.

knowledge.subject: Extinction
knowledge.description: the reduction of radiation through the combined effects of scattering and absorption.
knowledge.source: Extinction is the reduction of radiation through the combined effects of scattering and absorption.

knowledge.subject: Emission
knowledge.description: the release of radiation by matter into its surroundings.
knowledge.source: Emission is the release of radiation by matter into its surroundings.

knowledge.subject: Absorption (radiative process)
knowledge.description: removes radiant energy and converts it into internal heat.
knowledge.source: Absorption removes radiant energy and converts it into internal heat.

knowledge.subject: Absorptivity (α)
knowledge.description: The fraction absorbed
knowledge.source: The fraction absorbed is described by absorptivity (α).

knowledge.subject: Ozone absorption
knowledge.description: selectively absorbs ultraviolet radiation
knowledge.source: Ozone selectively absorbs ultraviolet radiation, while water vapor and carbon dioxide selectively absorb longwave infrared radiation.

knowledge.subject: Water vapor and carbon dioxide absorption
knowledge.description: selectively absorb longwave infrared radiation.
knowledge.source: Ozone selectively absorbs ultraviolet radiation, while water vapor and carbon dioxide selectively absorb longwave infrared radiation.

knowledge.subject: Radiation emission by objects
knowledge.description: Every object above absolute zero emits radiation.
knowledge.source: Every object above absolute zero emits radiation.

knowledge.subject: Emissivity (ε)
knowledge.description: measures the efficiency of real materials emitting radiation compared to perfect blackbodies, which is generally less than 1.
knowledge.source: Real materials emit less efficiently than perfect blackbodies, and this efficiency is measured by emissivity (ε), which is generally less than 1.

knowledge.subject: Scattering
knowledge.description: changes the direction of radiation without converting it into heat.
knowledge.source: Scattering changes the direction of radiation without converting it into heat.

knowledge.subject: Rayleigh scattering
knowledge.description: occurs with particles much smaller than the wavelength of light and strongly affects shorter wavelengths, producing blue skies and red sunsets.
knowledge.source: Rayleigh scattering occurs with particles much smaller than the wavelength of light and strongly affects shorter wavelengths, producing blue skies and red sunsets.

knowledge.subject: Mie scattering
knowledge.description: occurs with particles comparable in size to the wavelength and produces haze and whitish glare.
knowledge.source: Mie scattering occurs with particles comparable in size to the wavelength and produces haze and whitish glare.

knowledge.subject: Non-selective scattering
knowledge.description: occurs with particles much larger than the wavelength and affects all wavelengths equally, causing clouds to appear white.
knowledge.source: Non-selective scattering occurs with particles much larger than the wavelength and affects all wavelengths equally, causing clouds to appear white.

enumerations.title: Wavelength spans of different radiation types
items: Ultraviolet radiation spans wavelengths from 0.01 to 0.4 µm.
Thermal infrared radiation spans 0.7 to 100 µm.
